meaning: n.鍦ㄥ鍒ゅ墠鍒ゅ喅,棰勬柇
examples: There is an obvious prejudgment here that should be avoided.
Processing speed and power dissipation are well optimized for special inputs with prejudgment for input data.
Previewing records before court does not necessarily lead to a disadvantaged result for the defendant, it should be conducted for "precognition" of case information but not as a "prejudgment".
Our society can be awful long on prejudgment and woefully short on consequences.
With respect to prejudgment interest, the parties advocated different interest rates.
